commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=5250f1a2c4dc8937b1d81aee04184194f553403a)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds shared exec-like tool failure-warning formatting that labels the failed tool separately, strips some `run <binary>` subjects, appends concise exit-code suffixes, and updates embedded-runner payload tests.

PR surface: Source +118, Tests +35. Total +153 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main formats exec summaries such as `run python3 ...` and then wraps `lastToolError.meta` into `⚠️ ... failed`. I did not run a live cron/chat reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor-side real behavior proof plus a code revision for the generic run-summary gap; this should not enter the repair lane while the external proof gate is unsatisfied.

target.title: fix: clarify exec tool failure warnings
target.body:
Closes #97319

AI-assisted by Codex.

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users debugging failed exec/bash tool calls could see the framework-generated action summary rendered like a literal command, for example a Python script failure appearing as if the agent typed `run python3 ...`.

This affected chat/tool-failure cards and cron run diagnostics that reuse the embedded-runner payload warning text.

## Why This Change Was Made

Exec-like tool failures now render the tool label and failure state separately from the command subject, using forms like `Exec failed: <command>` instead of wrapping the whole summary before appending `failed`.

When the stored exec summary is a known interpreter-style `run python3 ...` display summary, the warning renders the literal command portion without the framework `run` label. Non-verbose exec failures also include a concise `(exit N)` suffix when the error text exposes an exit code. Arbitrary cwd suffixes remain inside markdown code spans so mention-looking path text stays inert.

## User Impact

Users get clearer failure messages when debugging failed scripts or shell commands. The change is presentation-only; it does not change tool execution, retry policy, cron behavior, or verbose error-detail rules.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts src/cron/run-diagnostics.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.helpers.test.ts src/agents/tool-display.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.test.ts`
- `node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --codex-bin /tmp/codex-fast-wrapper` reported clean after one accepted review finding was fixed and revalidated.
target.author: aditya-vithaldas
